content:
- Executed plan for role-specific credential usage across runtime/process/HA.
- Implemented typed conn wrappers for basebackup (replicator) and rewind (rewinder), plus bootstrap superuser username; removed stub-derived default leakage.
- Added process env plumbing for password auth via PGPASSWORD using SecretSource resolved at spawn-time; added validation for pg_rewind source fields.
- Enforced config cross-field invariants: local_conn_identity.user == roles.superuser.username, rewind_conn_identity.user == roles.rewinder.username; updated fixtures across repo.
- Updated HA e2e harness: thread configured superuser user/db into psql helper and provision replicator/rewinder roles on the primary after bootstrap so clones using -U replicator succeed.
- Gates passed: make check, make test, make test-long, make lint.
- Task marked done/passing and task_switch.sh executed.

- Read .ralph/current_tasks.md and searched for any <passes>false</passes>, <passing>false</passing>, and <passes>meta-task</passes> entries.
- No bug tasks were failing (no bug with <passes>false</passes> or <passing>false</passing>).
- Found a recurring meta-task (story-rust-system-harness/18) but per rules selected a <passes>false</passes> task instead.
- Chose next logical unblocked work item in the secure explicit config story chain: wire HTTP/PG TLS + pg_hba/pg_ident + DCS init into startup orchestration.
- Wrote selected task path to .ralph/current_task.txt.

- Scanned HTTP server startup/config wiring for API, focusing on runtime path, API worker TLS/auth code, parser/schema/defaults, and BDD/unit tests.
- Confirmed runtime currently binds API listener and starts worker but does not apply TLS config (cert/key, client_auth) into ApiWorkerCtx at startup. ApiWorkerCtx TLS and mTLS knobs exist but are only used in tests via configure_tls/set_require_client_cert.
- Confirmed auth wiring is active via cfg.api.security.auth (disabled or role tokens) with token trimming and endpoint role enforcement; no true legacy v1 auth fallback remains in production despite legacy-named tests.
- Confirmed parser enforces API security presence and TLS identity when mode optional/required, plus non-empty role tokens; but parser does not validate/consume client_auth.client_ca today.
- Next if returning in later context: implement runtime TLS builder from InlineOrPath for cert/key/(optional) client CA, wire into api_ctx, validate client_auth fields, add runtime integration tests for tls required/optional + mTLS and negative startup cases.

- Scanned runtime bootstrap/startup and process command build path for PostgreSQL TLS + pg_hba/pg_ident materialization.
- Confirmed config schema/parser includes explicit postgres.tls, postgres.pg_hba, postgres.pg_ident in v2 and validates tls identity when mode != disabled.
- Confirmed runtime startup path (runtime/node -> execute_startup -> process/worker build_command) does not consume postgres.tls/pg_hba/pg_ident at all; start command only sets host/port/socket via pg_ctl -o.
- Confirmed pg_hba/pg_ident are currently dead-config fields at runtime; no writing to data_dir/pg_hba.conf or pg_ident.conf, no hba_file/ident_file override, no reload/restart logic tied to these fields.
- Confirmed PostgreSQL server-side TLS config is not materialized during startup: no ssl=on/off, no ssl_cert_file/ssl_key_file/ssl_ca_file handling in runtime/process.
- Noted related gap: dcs.init (payload_json/write_on_bootstrap) also appears unused in startup path.
- Located existing pending task file .ralph/tasks/story-secure-explicit-node-config/04-task-wire-http-pg-tls-pg_hba-pg_ident-and-dcs-init-into-startup.md that matches this gap.
- Next step: provide user concise file map + concrete wiring plan + test plan (unit/integration/real-binary) scoped to startup/bootstrap behavior.

- Scanned DCS/bootstrap wiring end-to-end for config usage and write paths.
- Confirmed DcsConfig includes optional init block (payload_json, write_on_bootstrap) but no runtime path currently consumes it for writes.
- Enumerated actual DCS writes: DCS worker writes /<scope>/member/<self>, HA worker writes/deletes /<scope>/leader and deletes /<scope>/switchover, API controller writes/deletes /<scope>/switchover.
- Verified /<scope>/config and /<scope>/init are only parsed from watch events and reflected in DcsCache, not emitted by this node today.
- Collected specific files/lines for proposed wiring: likely runtime startup + HA bootstrap dispatch + DCS store helper trait expansions + parser validation for dcs.init payload/write_on_bootstrap.
- Next: provide user with suggested implementation steps and targeted tests (unit + integration/e2e) without making code edits yet.

- Read progress log (empty), then read `.ralph/current_task.txt` -> `.ralph/tasks/story-secure-explicit-node-config/04-task-wire-http-pg-tls-pg_hba-pg_ident-and-dcs-init-into-startup.md`.
- Spawned 3 explorer subagents to map HTTP TLS wiring, Postgres TLS + pg_hba/pg_ident wiring, and DCS init wiring. Confirmed all three are currently parsed/validated but not wired into runtime startup/orchestration.
- Identified key runtime gap: `src/runtime/node.rs` builds `ApiWorkerCtx` but never configures TLS acceptor/mTLS; startup `execute_startup` and process `StartPostgresSpec` don't apply postgres TLS or pg_hba/pg_ident; `dcs.init` exists but no bootstrap write uses it.
- Next: write a detailed multi-module plan into the task md, ending with `TO BE VERIFIED`, then stop (per task protocol).

- Performed deep skeptical review of API TLS wiring in runtime startup (no code changes).
- Read runtime/node startup + worker wiring, api/worker TLS accept path, config schema/parser/validation, debug view, process command builder, and TLS helpers.
- Confirmed critical gap: runtime creates ApiWorkerCtx but never configures tls acceptor nor require_client_cert from cfg.api.security.tls; with mode required/optional, API silently drops TLS handshakes because acceptor is missing, while run loop swallows step errors.
- Confirmed parser validates tls.identity when mode!=disabled but does not validate tls.client_auth.client_ca presence/non-empty or coupling with require_client_cert; runtime also does not consume client_auth at all.
- Confirmed only TLS server-config builders are in test_harness (test-only); production has no shared cert-loading helper from InlineOrPath to rustls ServerConfig.
- Confirmed observability mismatch: debug payload reports tls_enabled from config mode only, not from successfully loaded acceptor/mTLS state.
- Confirmed test gap: API TLS tests manually call ctx.configure_tls(...)/set_require_client_cert(true), and runtime/node tests do not assert config-driven TLS wiring.
- Next if resumed: propose concrete plan edits for validation + startup fail-fast + runtime integration tests + shared TLS loader architecture.

- Performed deep skeptical review on Postgres managed config + process pg_ctl -o plumbing.
- Inspected src/runtime/node.rs, src/process/worker.rs, src/process/jobs.rs, src/ha/worker.rs, src/ha/state.rs, config parser/schema, and current task plan doc.
- Confirmed current runtime/process path has zero production materialization helpers for pg_hba/pg_ident/TLS files; start/restart still hardcode single -o string `-h ... -p ... -k ...`.
- Identified plan gaps: wrong escaping layer for pg_ctl -o payload, missing deterministic ordering/key validation for -c settings, idempotence not fully specified (content hashing/fsync/atomic replace semantics), restart/reload semantics underspecified for running postgres and stale postmaster.pid, cross-platform gaps (socket `-k`, rename semantics, unix perms), and timeout cancellation only killing pg_ctl not postmaster.
- Next if continuing: tighten plan sections with explicit renderer contract + tests, add managed-config fingerprint + reload/restart policy, add robust stop fallback in production process layer, and split unix/non-unix behavior explicitly.

- Performed deep skeptical review of DCS init semantics: traced config schema/parser, DCS key parsing/decoding, etcd store capabilities, startup planner/executor, and HA dispatch actions.
- Confirmed dcs.init config exists in schema but is never consumed for writes. Only member/leader/switchover are written today.
- Confirmed /config and /init are decoded from watch events into DcsCache, but there is no writer API for them and no bootstrap flow hook.
- Found semantic mismatch risk: /config decode expects RuntimeConfig while dcs.init.payload_json implies arbitrary JSON bootstrap payload.
- Found safety gap: startup InitializePrimary decision ignores init lock evidence and relies only on leader/member, so empty nodes can choose initialize even when cluster is already initialized but leader is temporarily absent.
- Logged bug via add-bug skill: .ralph/tasks/bugs/dcs-init-config-key-bootstrap-semantics-not-implemented.md
- Next if continuing: implement explicit bootstrap metadata write contract (init CAS + optional config write), add transactional etcd operation support, wire startup decision/execution to honor init lock, and add deterministic unit + real-etcd transaction tests without watch-timing dependence.

- Read .ralph/current_task.txt -> .ralph/tasks/story-secure-explicit-node-config/04-task-wire-http-pg-tls-pg_hba-pg_ident-and-dcs-init-into-startup.md.
- Task file was in TO BE VERIFIED state; ran deep skeptical verification using 3 explorer subagents (API TLS, PG start plumbing, DCS init semantics).
- Key plan corrections applied in the task doc:
  - API TLS: add config validation for client_auth vs mode, require client_ca when mTLS required, and require runtime-path tests (node startup wiring) not just ApiWorkerCtx unit tests.
  - Postgres start: replaced incorrect “GUC string literal escaping” with an explicit pg_ctl -o token rendering/escaping contract; added key validation + deterministic ordering guidance to prevent option injection and flakiness.
  - DCS init: tightened semantics to be race-safe by introducing CAS/txn-style put-if-absent primitives; require init_lock to gate InitializePrimary; claim /<scope>/init write-once before bootstrap; seed /<scope>/config only if absent; updated etcd tests to assert transaction results rather than watch timing.
- Replaced final marker in the plan from TO BE VERIFIED -> NOW EXECUTE.
- Noted that a bug file exists at .ralph/tasks/bugs/dcs-init-config-key-bootstrap-semantics-not-implemented.md (created earlier in repo) for missing init semantics; current task plan now incorporates the critical safety pieces (init lock + txn).

- Executed task 04-task-wire-http-pg-tls-pg_hba-pg_ident-and-dcs-init-into-startup.md (state NOW EXECUTE).
- Wired managed Postgres config into startup + HA StartPostgres: new `src/postgres_managed.rs` materializes `pgtm.pg_hba.conf`, `pgtm.pg_ident.conf`, and optional TLS PEMs, and passes `-c hba_file=...`/`ident_file=...`/`ssl_*` via `pg_ctl -o`.
- Extended process layer: `StartPostgresSpec`/`RestartPostgresSpec` now carry deterministic `extra_postgres_settings` (BTreeMap) and command builder renders `-c key=value` tokens safely; added unit tests.
- Wired API TLS into runtime workers: new `src/tls.rs` builds rustls ServerConfig from InlineOrPath PEM identity + optional client CA; runtime configures ApiWorkerCtx acceptor + client-cert requirement.
- Implemented DCS init bootstrap: runtime claims `/<scope>/init` via etcd txn (put-if-absent) and seeds `/<scope>/config` from `dcs.init.payload_json` only if absent; startup-mode planning avoids InitializePrimary when init lock exists.
- Added/updated real-binary tests: HA e2e harness asserts SHOW hba_file/ident_file point to managed files and etcd has init/config keys; fixed pg_hba to include `host replication` so pg_basebackup works.
- Updated config validation to require non-empty pg_hba/pg_ident and coherent TLS client_auth; updated fixtures/examples accordingly.
- All gates passing: `make check`, `make test`, `make test-long`, `make lint`.
- Next: switch task + commit/push; consider future tightening for Postgres tls.mode=required semantics (enforce hostssl-only HBA) if desired.
